title: AI Risk Mapping
description: The organization identifies and documents risks associated with the AI system across its lifecycle, including risks to individuals, groups, and society.

title: Data Governance
description: Data used for AI training and inference is governed with policies addressing quality, provenance, privacy, and representativeness.

title: Transparency and Explainability
description: The AI system provides meaningful explanations of its decisions to relevant stakeholders, proportionate to the level of risk.

title: Bias and Fairness Testing
description: The organization tests the AI system for harmful biases across demographic groups and documents mitigation strategies.

title: Human Oversight
description: Mechanisms are in place for human review and override of AI decisions, especially in high-stakes contexts.

title: Security and Resilience
description: The AI system is protected against adversarial attacks, data poisoning, and model extraction, with incident response plans.

title: Privacy Preservation
description: The AI system minimizes collection and retention of personal data and applies privacy-enhancing technologies where appropriate.

title: Model Validation and Testing
description: The AI model is validated through rigorous testing including accuracy, robustness, and performance under edge cases before deployment.

title: Accountability and Documentation
description: Roles, responsibilities, and decision-making processes for the AI system are clearly documented and assigned.

title: Continuous Monitoring
description: The AI system is monitored post-deployment for performance degradation, drift, and emerging risks, with defined escalation procedures.
